paper_title: Bee Abundance and Distribution in Crape Myrtle Plantings

methods.description: Setup of the field experiment to study bee abundance and distribution in crape myrtle plantings.

steps.description: The study was conducted at the University of Florida's Institute of Food and Agricultural Sciences (UF/IFAS), North Florida Research & Education Center (NFREC-Quincy) in Quincy, Florida.
steps.searchable_summary: set up field experiment location at UF/IFAS NFREC-Quincy

steps.description: The block of 224 crape myrtles was divided into 56 quadrats, each containing 4 crape myrtles of the same cultivar.
steps.searchable_summary: divide crape myrtle block into quadrats

steps.description: Plant spacing was 6 m between and 5 m within the rows.
steps.searchable_summary: set plant spacing for crape myrtles

methods.description: Monitoring bee activity and collecting data on bee abundance and distribution in crape myrtle plantings.

steps.description: Transect walks were made through all quadrats in a serpentine fashion on several dates to monitor pollinator activity.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct transect walks to monitor bee activity

steps.description: Bee collections via netting were made from the entire planting to assess accuracy of identifications.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bees using netting for identification

steps.description: Percentage bloom was estimated visually for each cultivar on each date.

methods.description: Identification and verification of bee species collected during the study.

steps.description: Species were identified using a Leica MZ12.5 scope equipped with a Leica DFC 295 camera and Discover Life online keys.
steps.searchable_summary: identify bee species using microscope and online keys

steps.description: Where identifications were in question, key characters were imaged and posted to the Bee Monitoring Network for verification.
steps.searchable_summary: verify bee identifications via imaging and online network

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data on bee abundance and distribution.

steps.description: All analyses were conducted using SAS or ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using SAS and ArcGIS

steps.description: Least squares means (LSMeans) with Tukey adjustment for multiple comparisons was used in SAS PROC GLM to analyze the effects of cultivar.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze effects of cultivar using LSMeans in SAS

steps.description: Spatial pattern was evaluated by constructing abundance-by-location maps using inverse distance weighting in ESRI ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: create abundance maps using inverse distance weighting in ArcGIS
